message:
🌱 distribution: Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes multi-arch manifests (#791)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "distribution-3178-registry-garbage-collect-delete-untagged-removes-multi-arch-ma",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "distribution: Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes multi-arch manifests",
+    "description": "Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes multi-arch manifests. Requested by 17+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current distribution deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your distribution version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
helm list -n distribution 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working distribution installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review distribution configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution
```
Hello!
We are currently experimenting with using multi-arch images in our infrastructure, as we have a mix of IBM Power and regular AMD64-based machines, and are currently using different tags/repositories to distinguish between the architectures,"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes…",
+        "description": "Updates the Manifest and ManifestBuilder to distinguish between blob and manifest references. Uses this new interface to fix the garbage collection logic to do a proper transitive closure on references so that untagged sub manifests referenced by (tagged) manifest lists do not get removed even if
```yaml
# Remove old registry, clean slate
docker rm -f registry
docker system prune --all --volumes -f

# Start registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry

# Pull AMD64-image
docker pull "docker"
docker tag "docker" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"

# ARM-image
docker pull "ppc64le/docker"
docker tag "ppc64le/docker" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"

#Create and push manifest for multi-arch ubuntu
docker manifest create --insecure "localhost:5000/docker:latest" "localhost:50
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Updates the Manifest and ManifestBuilder to distinguish between blob and manifest references. Uses this new interface to fix the garbage collection logic to do a proper transitive closure on references so that untagged sub manifests referenced by (tagged) manifest lists do not get removed even if remove-untagged is given to the garbage collector.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Remove old registry, clean slate
docker rm -f registry
docker system prune --all --volumes -f

# Start registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry

# Pull AMD64-image
docker pull "docker"
docker tag "docker" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"

# ARM-image
docker pull "ppc64le/docker"
docker tag "ppc64le/docker" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"

#Create and push manifest for multi-arch ubuntu
docker manifest create --insecure "localhost:5000/docker:latest" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker manifest push -p "localhost:5000/docker:latest"

# Clear local cache
docker system prune --all -f

# Garbage-collect registry
dock",
